 {"id":"frankentorch-jhi","title":"Make Optimizer::set_momentum return Result instead of silent no-op","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found that `Optimizer::set_momentum(&mut self, _momentum: f64)` in ft-optim/src/lib.rs:111 has an empty body `{}`. This is the ONLY empty function body in the entire codebase.

The paired `get_momentum()` default returns `None`, which is correct. But `set_momentum()` silently discards the value with no feedback — a caller setting momentum on an Adagrad optimizer (which does not support momentum) gets no error and no indication that their intent was ignored.

## Problem
Silent data loss violates the principle of least surprise. In PyTorch, calling an unsupported setter is either a no-op with a warning or raises an error depending on the optimizer. FrankenTorch should be at least as explicit.

## Plan
1. Change signature to `fn set_momentum(&mut self, _momentum: f64) -> Result<(), AutogradError>`
2. Default implementation returns `Err(optimizer_hparam_error("this optimizer does not support momentum"))`
3. SGD (and any other momentum-supporting optimizer) overrides to actually set the value and return `Ok(())`
4. Update all callers to handle the Result
5. Add a test verifying that non-momentum optimizers reject set_momentum

## Acceptance Criteria
- `set_momentum` on Adagrad/RMSprop/RAdam returns descriptive Err
- `set_momentum` on SGD succeeds
- All existing optimizer tests pass
- Zero empty function bodies remain in the codebase

## Dependencies
None — this is an independent API change within ft-optim.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:22:34.711976324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:45:29.961692699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:45:29.961433909Z","close_reason":"Optimizer::set_momentum now returns an explicit unsupported-feature error by default, SGD implements Result-based momentum updates, OneCycleLR handles failures visibly, and ft-optim tests plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
